narrative: Easily test the karat content of gold with ToolTreaux’s 18K Gold Testing Kit with Testing Stone and Neutralizer Combo! If testing fine jewelry or other finished gold items, be sure to choose an inconspicuous spot, so as not to compromise the finished piece. INSTRUCTIONS: To test gold karat content, scratch the item onto the test stone leaving a visible deposit. Place a small drop of solution on top of the deposit; if the gold is the same karat or higher, the solution will not affect the deposit. If the deposit is of a lower karat, the deposit will dissolve and eventually disappear. When done, add an equal amount of neutralizing solution to neutralize the old solution. Ensure gold testing stone has been thoroughly washed and neutralized before starting any more testing! PLEASE NOTE: Testing solutions can be harmful to the skin. Please exercise the utmost caution when opening and using this product and method of testing! Proper protective eye wear and gloves are highly recommended, as the solution can burn unprotected skin. Ensure neutralization of the testing solution after every test by using the neutralizing solution. Read the label on the bottle and follow provided instructions.

SILVER TESTING KIT METHOD: This silver testing method is best used on silver that is yet to be smelted, due to the harsh nature of the solution that could potentially damage an already finished item; if testing fine jewelry, sterling silver items or other finished items, be sure to choose an inconspicuous spot so as not to compromise the finished piece HOW THE SILVER TESTING KIT WORKS: To test silver purity, scratch the item onto the test stone leaving a visible deposit, then place a small drop of the solution over top of deposit; the purity of the silver is approximated according to the color the solution turns when it is applied to the sample; if the solution turns bright red that indicates high quality silver; if the solution turns blue or green the sample may contain brass or copper; if the sample deposit dissolves quickly, or the solution turns a dark brown or yellow, it is likely a base metal or silver plated

narrative: If you need to test silver, gold, or platinum quality frequently, then you absolutely need a reliable set of acid-testing solutions to make sure you’re identifying the quality of your precious metals accurately. The ToolTreaux acid testing kit comes with bottles of testing acid solutions and a testing stone to identify the quality of your silver.

To test content and purity, scratch the item onto the test stone leaving a visible deposit, then place a small drop of the solution over top of deposit. Ensure gold testing stone has been thoroughly washed before completing any testing!

The Silver Quality solution is designed specifically to indicate whether your silver is high-quality or low-quality. A cream color indicates high quality, usually sterling; black color indicates coin quality, green color indicates high amounts of copper/poor quality; gold color indicates brass/silver plated item.

The Gold and Platinum solution is designed to specifically indicate whether your material is legitimate. If the gold is the same karat or higher, the solution will not affect the deposit; if the deposit is of a lower karat, the deposit will dissolve and eventually disappear Test solutions work best in small quantities, so it’s recommended to use the neutralizing solution in small amounts for each test. Since we’ve included 2 bottles, this amount should last through many tests. Please note that this testing solution is not used to differentiate between different types of silver (example: .999, .925, .80) The silver testing kit with a testing stone is ideal for jewelry makers and sellers, coin collectors and sellers, and metalworking enthusiasts and appraisers alike! Each bottle contains .5 ounces of testing solution and the stone measures 2.25” x 1.75”. SAFETY NOTE: Acid Testing Solutions can be harmful to the skin. Please exercise the utmost caution when opening and using this product and method of testing! Proper protective eye wear and gloves are highly recommended, as the solution can burn unprotected skin. Read the label on the bottle and follow the included operational instructions.

short_description: Easily test the karat content of gold, as well as the purity of silver and platinum, with ToolTreaux’s Complete Gold Testing Kit. The kit includes six, 0.5oz shatterproof plastic squeeze bottles (10k, 14k, 18k, 22k, silver and platinum) and one testing stone; the testing solutions are potent, effective and will see you through hundreds of tests! narrative: Easily test the karat content of gold, as well as the purity of silver and platinum, with ToolTreaux’s Complete Gold Testing Kit. The kit includes six, 0.5oz shatterproof plastic squeeze bottles (10k, 14k, 18k, 22k, silver and platinum) and one testing stone; the testing solutions are potent, effective and will see you through hundreds of tests! A must-have tool for gold panning, coin and jewelry appraisal or other professions that work with gold and precious metals. And, housed in small, sturdy, shatterproof plastic squeeze bottles, the testing solution bottles are comfortable to maneuver, while also easily maintaining control over the amount of solution escaping the bottle. TESTING SOLUTION METHOD: This testing method is ideally used on unfinished gold, silver and platinum pieces, due to the harsh nature of the solution that could potentially damage an already finished item; however, if testing fine jewelry or other finished items, choose an inconspicuous spot so as not to compromise finishing TESTING FOR GOLD: To test for karat content or purity, scratch the item on to the test stone leaving a visible deposit, then place a small drop of the solution over top of deposit; if gold is the same karat or higher the solution will not affect the deposit; if the deposit is of a lower karat, the deposit will dissolve TESTING FOR PLATINUM: For platinum repeat the same process as above; if the material is pure platinum, the deposit will not be affected and the solution will not change TESTING FOR SILVER: For silver, repeat the same process as above; if the material is of high quality silver, the solution will turn bright red; if the solution turns blue or green the sample may contain brass or copper; if the sample deposit dissolves quickly, or the solution turns a dark brown or yellow, it is likely a base metal PLEASE NOTE: Testing Solutions can be harmful to the skin. Please exercise the utmost caution when opening and using this product and method of testing! Proper protective eye wear and gloves are highly recommended, as the solution can burn unprotected skin. Read the label on the bottle and follow the included operational instructions.

PLATINUM TESTING KIT METHOD: This platinum testing method is best used on raw platinum nuggets or grains, due to the harsh nature of the solution that could potentially damage an already finished item; if testing fine jewelry or other finished items, be sure to choose an inconspicuous spot so as not to compromise a finished piece HOW THE PLATINUM TESTING KIT WORKS: To test for platinum purity, scratch the item onto the test stone leaving a visible deposit, then place a small drop of the solution over top of deposit; if the material on the scratch stone is platinum, the deposit should keep its bright white color for up to a minute; ensure the testing stone has been thoroughly washed before performing any purity tests SAFETY NOTE: Testing Solutions can be harmful to the skin. Please exercise the utmost caution when opening and using this product and method of testing! Proper protective eye wear and gloves are highly recommended, as the solution can burn unprotected skin. Read the label on the bottle and follow the included operational instructions.
